text: Seven new features, 25 bug fixes, seven improvements. Version 2.1.126 shipped today. The biggest cluster, auth and sessions, four fixes landing together. First, WSL2, SSH, and dev content users can now paste the OAuth code directly in the terminal when the browser callback cannot reach local host. Second, OAuth timeouts on slow and ip6-only connections fixed. Third, stream idle timeout after waking your Mac from sleep patched. And fourth, background and remote sessions no longer abort on long thinking pauses. Six changes that matter across this release. Run Claude project purged to wipe all project state, transcripts, tasks, file history, with a dry run flag to preview first. The dangerously skipped permissions flag now bypasses dot Claude, dot git, dot vscode, and shell config writes without interruption. OAuth works in the terminal now. No browser redirect required. The slash model picker lists every model your gateway exposes via slash v1 slash models. On Windows, Claude now treats PowerShell as the primary shell when the PowerShell tool is enabled. And images larger than 2000 pixels are downscaled on paste. No more session crashes. Enterprise and SDK. If you are on a custom gateway, the slash model picker reads your models list automatically from slash v1 slash models. Host managed deployments on bedrock, vertex, and foundry no longer auto disable analytics when provider managed by host is set. The domain allow list security policy now enforces correctly even when a higher priority setting source lacks a sandbox block. And the agent SDK no longer hangs when the model emits a malformed tool name in a parallel tool call batch. Ship it. More on auth. A race condition that could clear a valid OAuth refresh token during concurrent credential writes, patched. The login screen for organization OAuth errors now shows guidance to contact your admin instead of a dead end. Deferred tools, web search, web fetch, now load correctly for skills with context fork on their first turn. And the API retry countdown no longer sticks at zero seconds between retries. MCP and remote control. Claw.ai MCP connectors were being suppressed by manual servers stuck in needs auth state that is fixed. Remote control retries now show their result on each attempt instead of freezing on connecting. Remote control failure notifications now include the actual error reason for the initial connection failure and plan mode tools are now available in interactive sessions launched with the channel's flag. Terminal and windows. Overly fast trackpad scrolling and cursor and VS code versions 1.92 through 1.104. Fixed. Japanese, Korean and Chinese text rendering as garbled characters on windows in no flicker mode. Also fixed. Control L now redraws the screen instead of clearing your prompt matching read line behavior. And windows clipboard writes no longer leak copied content into process arguments visible to security tooling. Selections above 22 kilobytes also now reach the clipboard. A few more quality of life changes. In auto mode, the spinner now turns red when a permission checks stalls. So you know clawed is waiting, not running. The file modified reminder is now bounded when a linter touches many files at once. Slash plugin uninstall now correctly reports uninstalled instead of enabled. And in PowerShell, a bare double dash like in git diff dash dash file is no longer misflagged as a stop parsing token. To pull every fix from this release, run clawed update. That is it. Drop a like, comment which fix you needed most and subscribe for the next version. Which one made your day? Tell me below.
